 {"id":"bd-28cpd","title":"PromptInjector: Handle Codex double-Enter quirk","description":"## Purpose
Handle Codex-specific quirk that requires double-Enter to submit prompts, ensuring reliable prompt injection across all agent types.

## Implementation Details

```go
// AgentQuirks defines agent-specific prompt submission behaviors
type AgentQuirks struct {
    DoubleEnter    bool   // Codex requires double Enter
    EscapePrefix   bool   // Some agents need Escape before input
    ClearLineFirst bool   // Clear existing input before prompt
}

var agentQuirksMap = map[string]AgentQuirks{
    "cod": {DoubleEnter: true},
    "cc":  {DoubleEnter: false},
    "gmi": {DoubleEnter: false},
}

// SendWithQuirks sends prompt handling agent-specific requirements
func (pi *PromptInjector) SendWithQuirks(paneID, agentType, prompt string) error {
    quirks := agentQuirksMap[agentType]
    
    pi.logger.Debug("applying agent quirks",
        "pane", paneID,
        "agent_type", agentType,
        "double_enter", quirks.DoubleEnter)
    
    // Clear line if needed
    if quirks.ClearLineFirst {
        if err := pi.tmux.SendKeys(paneID, "C-u", false); err != nil {
            return fmt.Errorf("clear line: %w", err)
        }
    }
    
    // Send the prompt text
    if err := pi.tmux.SendKeys(paneID, prompt, true); err != nil {
        return fmt.Errorf("send prompt: %w", err)
    }
    
    // Handle Codex double-Enter quirk
    if quirks.DoubleEnter {
        pi.logger.Debug("sending extra Enter for Codex", "pane", paneID)
        time.Sleep(50 * time.Millisecond) // Brief pause between Enters
        if err := pi.tmux.SendKeys(paneID, "", true); err != nil {
            return fmt.Errorf("send second enter: %w", err)
        }
    }
    
    pi.logger.Info("prompt sent with quirks applied",
        "pane", paneID,
        "agent_type", agentType)
    return nil
}
```

## Logging Requirements
- Log which quirks are being applied per agent type
- Debug-level log for double-Enter sends
- Info-level confirmation on successful send

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Codex prompts reliably submitted with double-Enter
- [ ] Quirks map is extensible for future agent types
- [ ] Small delay between Enters to ensure reliable submission
- [ ] No quirks applied to cc/gmi agents
- [ ] Unit tests verify quirk application per agent type","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-22T07:00:39.308715362Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T02:40:03.952997406Z","closed_at":"2026-01-23T02:40:03.952294280Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-28cpd","depends_on_id":"bd-17zih","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-22T07:18:55.075460619Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-28cpd","depends_on_id":"bd-3lo54","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-22T07:19:18.350206855Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-28n0d","title":"Implement shell environment detection","description":"Implement shell environment detection.

## What

Detect shell type and common configuration that affects agent operation.

## Output Schema

```json
{
  "shell": {
    "type": "zsh",              // bash, zsh, fish, etc.
    "tmux_plugin_detected": true,  // May cause issues
    "oh_my_zsh_detected": true,    // Common zsh framework
    "config_path": "~/.zshrc"    // Where to look for aliases
  }
}
```

## Detection Methods

### Shell Type
- Read $SHELL environment variable
- Verify against running shell process

### tmux Plugin Detection
Check common plugin locations:
- ~/.oh-my-zsh/plugins/tmux
- ~/.zshrc contains 'plugins=(... tmux ...)'
- ~/.tmux.conf sourced plugins

### oh-my-zsh Detection
- Check for ~/.oh-my-zsh directory
- Check ZSH environment variable

## Why This Matters

tmux plugins can:
- Alias the tmux command
- Add automatic session attachment
- Modify key bindings

Agents need to know to use /usr/bin/tmux directly when plugins detected.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:40:42.601068157Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T05:20:17.787668899Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T05:20:17.787582285Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-28n0d","depends_on_id":"bd-18gwh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:40:42.629141697Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-28o9m","title":"Task: Implement 'ntm ensemble modes' command for listing reasoning modes","description":"# Task: Implement 'ntm ensemble modes' command for listing/browsing reasoning modes

## Command Structure
```
ntm ensemble modes [subcommand] [flags]

Subcommands:
  list    List modes (default)
  show    Show full details for a single mode
```

## List Command
```
ntm ensemble modes list [flags]
  --category string   Filter by category (e.g., Formal, Causal)
  --tier string       Filter by tier (core|advanced|experimental)
  --all               Alias for --tier all (show all tiers)
  --format string     Output format: table, json, yaml (default: table)
  --verbose           Show full descriptions + failure modes
```

### Default Behavior
- **Default list = core tier only** (curated).
- If advanced modes exist, print a hint: `Use --all to show advanced modes`.

## Show Command
```
ntm ensemble modes show <mode-id|code> [flags]
  --format string     Output format: text, json, yaml (default: text)
```

## Behavior
- `ntm ensemble modes` with no subcommand defaults to `list`.
- Accepts lookup by **mode id** (lowercase) and **mode code** (e.g., A1).
- JSON/YAML outputs include: id, code, tier, name, category, short_desc, outputs, best_for, failure_modes, differentiator, icon, color.

## File Location
internal/cli/ensemble_modes.go

## Dependencies
- Requires mode catalog loader (bd-qn9gc)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Default list shows only core tier
- [ ] Category + tier filters work
- [ ] Show displays full mode details
- [ ] JSON/YAML outputs include code + tier
- [ ] `modes` defaults to `list`","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T07:39:23.818764532Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T01:53:42.069282507Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T01:53:42.069258051Z","close_reason":"Implemented as 'ntm modes' command (not 'ntm ensemble modes'). Functionality complete: ntm modes list with --category, --tier, --all, --format flags; ntm modes explain for mode details. All acceptance criteria met.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-28o9m","depends_on_id":"bd-qn9gc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-23T09:30:47.693780272Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-297mu","title":"Task: Add robot preflight command using DCG","description":"## Overview
Expose a robot-first command to preflight an arbitrary shell command via DCG.

## Proposed Interface
- `ntm --robot-guard --cmd "rm -rf /tmp"`
- Output: {success, allowed, reason, dcg_version}

- Fails gracefully if dcg missing

- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

 {"id":"bd-29dh","title":"Task: Implement one-click replay","description":"## Purpose
Enable replaying a prompt to a new or existing agent with one action.

## Scope
- Add replay action to history TUI (Enter or 'r' key)
- Options: replay to new agent, replay to specific agent, replay with modifications
- Track replay lineage for debugging

## Acceptance Criteria
- Can replay any historical prompt
- Replay spawns new agent or sends to existing
- Can edit prompt before replay
- Replay history tracks parent prompt

## Technical Notes
- Reuse spawn/send logic from existing commands
- Consider prompt templates for common modifications","notes":"Assume existing history storage; focus on UI/UX replay flow only.

## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

 {"id":"bd-29kr","title":"Add session profiles - save spawn config + prompts as named profile for quick reuse","description":"Add session profiles - save spawn config + prompts as named profile for quick reuse.

## Problem Being Solved

Users frequently spawn sessions with same configuration. Profiles allow saving and reusing spawn configs.

## Command Design

### Save Profile
```bash
ntm profile save PROFILE_NAME [--from SESSION]
ntm profile save myproject --cc=3 --cod=2 --prompt-file init.md
```

### Use Profile
```bash
ntm spawn SESSION --profile PROFILE_NAME
ntm spawn daily_work --profile myproject
```

### List Profiles
```bash
ntm profile list
```

### Delete Profile
```bash
ntm profile delete PROFILE_NAME
```

## Profile Storage
~/.config/ntm/profiles/PROFILE_NAME.toml

```toml
[spawn]
cc = 3
cod = 2
gmi = 0

[prompts]
init_prompt_file = "init.md"

[options]
auto_accept = true
```

## Robot Mode API
ntm --robot-profile-list
ntm --robot-profile-show=PROFILE_NAME

## Acceptance Criteria

1. ntm profile save creates profile file in ~/.config/ntm/profiles/
2. ntm profile list shows all saved profiles
3. ntm profile delete removes profile
4. ntm spawn --profile loads profile and uses config
5. Profile config overridden by explicit flags
6. Robot mode returns JSON for list/show
7. Profile file uses TOML format

## Unit Tests (80% coverage target)
- internal/cmd/profile_test.go
- Test save/load/delete operations
- Test profile merging with flags

## E2E Test Script
```bash
#!/bin/bash
# [E2E-PROFILES] Session Profiles Tests
LOG_PREFIX="[E2E-PROFILES]"
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Testing session profiles"
ntm profile save test_profile --cc=2 --cod=1
ntm profile list | grep test_profile || exit 1
ntm profile delete test_profile
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Profiles test complete"
```","acceptance_criteria":"1. Session profiles save spawn config
2. Profiles include agent counts and prompts
3. Named profiles stored in config directory
4. ntm spawn --profile NAME loads profile
5. ntm profile list shows available profiles
6. ntm profile save NAME creates profile
7. Unit tests for profile serialization
8. E2E test for profile creation and loading","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.
","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:36:30.444055670Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T00:34:31.981740710Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-29kr","depends_on_id":"bd-2b35","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-26T00:34:31.980213704Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3lm7w","title":"rust_proxy: Add --robot-proxy-status command","description":"Add robot mode command for rust_proxy status.

## Command Specification

```bash
ntm --robot-proxy-status
```

## Output Schema

```json
{
  "proxy": {
    "daemon_running": true,
    "version": "1.0.0",
    "listen_port": 8080,
    "uptime_seconds": 86400,
    "routes": [
      {
        "domain": "api.anthropic.com",
        "upstream": "proxy-us.example.com:443",
        "active": true,
        "bytes_in": 1000000,
        "bytes_out": 5000000,
        "requests": 500,
        "errors": 2
      }
    ],
    "failover_events": []
  }
}
```

## Code Location
- internal/robot/proxy_status.go

## Acceptance Criteria

1. --robot-proxy-status flag registered in root.go
2. Returns JSON with proxy daemon status
3. Returns daemon_running: false if proxy not running
4. Includes route statistics (bytes, requests, errors)
5. Includes failover events history
6. Returns error JSON if rust_proxy binary not found

## Unit Tests (80% coverage target)

- Test with mock proxy daemon
- Test daemon not running case

## E2E Test Script

```","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T07:44:50.933139692Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T18:21:54.655887391Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3lm7w","depends_on_id":"bd-2zkfw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T07:44:50.962596690Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3lo54","title":"EPIC: Weighted Multi-Project Agent Swarm Orchestration","description":"## Overview

This epic introduces a fully automated multi-project agent swarm orchestration system that intelligently allocates coding agents (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini) across multiple projects based on their open bead backlogs.

## Problem Statement

When managing multiple projects in /dp, each with varying amounts of work (tracked via beads), manually calculating agent allocation, creating tmux sessions, launching agents, and handling usage limits is tedious and error-prone. We need a single command that:

1. Scans projects for open bead counts
2. Calculates weighted agent allocation (more beads = more agents)
3. Creates tmux sessions with correct pane geometry
4. Launches agents (cc, cod, gmi) in each pane
5. Sends marching orders to all agents
6. Monitors for usage limit hits
7. Auto-respawns with account rotation when limits hit

## Key Features

### 1. Bead-Weighted Allocation
- Scan /dp projects (or specified list) for open bead counts via `br list --status open --json`
- Apply tiered allocation formula:
  - 400+ beads: 4 cc, 4 cod, 2 gmi agents
  - 100-399 beads: 3 cc, 3 cod, 2 gmi agents
  - <100 beads: 1 cc, 1 cod, 1 gmi agents
- Configurable thresholds and ratios

### 2. Session Orchestration
- Create 3 sessions per agent type: {type}_agents_1, {type}_agents_2, {type}_agents_3
- Distribute panes evenly across sessions of same type
- Ensure identical pane geometry across sessions
- Support remote execution via SSH

### 3. Usage Limit Detection
- Monitor pane output for limit patterns:
  - CC: "You've hit your limit"
  - COD: "You've hit your usage limit"
- Configurable pattern matching
- Real-time monitoring via background goroutines

### 4. Auto-Respawn with Account Rotation
- When limit detected: kill agent, rotate to next account, respawn
- Integration with caam (coding_agent_account_manager) for account switching
- Configurable retry limits and backoff

### 5. Marching Orders Injection
- Send standardized initial prompt to all agents
- Staggered sending to avoid rate limits
- Codex double-Enter quirk handling

## Technical Approach

### New CLI Command: `ntm swarm`

```bash
# Basic usage - scan /dp and auto-allocate
ntm swarm --scan-dir=/dp

# Specify projects explicitly
ntm swarm --projects=flywheel_connectors,ntm,asupersync

# Custom allocation formula
ntm swarm --scan-dir=/dp --tier1-threshold=400 --tier2-threshold=100

# Dry run to preview allocation
ntm swarm --scan-dir=/dp --dry-run

# With account rotation enabled
ntm swarm --scan-dir=/dp --auto-rotate-accounts

# Robot mode for automation
ntm --robot-swarm --scan-dir=/dp
```

### New Internal Packages

1. `internal/swarm/` - Core orchestration logic
2. `internal/beadscanner/` - Project bead counting
3. `internal/limitdetect/` - Usage limit pattern matching
4. `internal/accountrotate/` - Account rotation integration

### Configuration

New config section in `config.toml`:

```toml
[swarm]
default_scan_dir = "/dp"
tier1_threshold = 400  # beads
tier2_threshold = 100
tier1_allocation = { cc = 4, cod = 4, gmi = 2 }
tier2_allocation = { cc = 3, cod = 3, gmi = 2 }
tier3_allocation = { cc = 1, cod = 1, gmi = 1 }
sessions_per_type = 3
stagger_delay_ms = 300
auto_rotate_accounts = false

[swarm.limit_patterns]
cc = ["You've hit your limit", "usage limit"]
cod = ["You've hit your usage limit"]
gmi = ["quota exceeded"]

[swarm.marching_orders]
default = "First read ALL of the AGENTS.md file..."
review = "I want you to sort of randomly explore..."
```

## Success Criteria

1. Single command spawns weighted agent swarm across projects
2. Agents auto-start with marching orders
3. Usage limits detected within 30 seconds
4. Auto-respawn works with account rotation
5. Robot mode outputs JSON for automation
6. Full integration with existing NTM features (health, resilience, agent-mail)

## Dependencies

- Existing: spawn, send, health, resilience, config, tmux packages
- External: br (beads_rust), caam (coding_agent_account_manager)

## Out of Scope (for this epic)

- GUI/TUI for swarm management (future enhancement)
- Cloud-based account rotation (use local caam)
- Cross-machine swarm coordination (single machine focus)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-22T06:56:54.050264582Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T00:38:02.407074656Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3lo54","depends_on_id":"bd-1bdxp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-26T00:38:02.405085449Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-3mdhe","title":"Task: Implement mechanical merging algorithms for findings/recommendations","description":"# Task: Mechanical Merging Algorithms

Algorithmic pre-processing before synthesis agent runs.

## Purpose
Because all mode outputs follow OutputSchema, we can do significant mechanical merging before the synthesis agent:
1. Reduce token cost for synthesis agent
2. Surface patterns algorithmically
3. Pre-group related items

## Implementation
```go
// internal/ensemble/merge.go
type MechanicalMerger struct {
    Outputs []ModeOutput
}

type MergeResult struct {
    GroupedFindings      []FindingGroup
    GroupedRisks         []RiskGroup
    GroupedRecommendations []RecommendationGroup
    IdentifiedConflicts  []PotentialConflict
    Statistics           MergeStats
}

type FindingGroup struct {
    EvidencePointer string
    Findings        []Finding
    Modes           []string
}

type PotentialConflict struct {
    Topic     string
    ModeA     string
    ModeB     string
    PositionA string
    PositionB string
}

type MergeStats struct {
    TotalFindings    int
    UniqueFindings   int
    DuplicateRate    float64
    ConflictCount    int
}

func NewMechanicalMerger(outputs []ModeOutput) *MechanicalMerger
func (m *MechanicalMerger) Merge() (*MergeResult, error)

// Grouping algorithms
func (m *MechanicalMerger) groupFindingsByEvidence() []FindingGroup
func (m *MechanicalMerger) groupRisksBySeverity() []RiskGroup
func (m *MechanicalMerger) groupRecommendationsByAction() []RecommendationGroup
func (m *MechanicalMerger) detectConflicts() []PotentialConflict

// Similarity functions
func jaccardSimilarity(a, b string) float64
func evidenceProximity(a, b string) float64  // Same file = high, nearby lines = medium
```

## Conflict Detection Heuristics
- Contradictory theses (opposite sentiment on same topic)
- Risk severity disagreement (same risk, different severity)
- Recommendation conflicts (do X vs don't do X)

## File: internal/ensemble/merge.go","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T17:57:40.755425449Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T19:33:30.301464091Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3mdhe","depends_on_id":"bd-2qwm8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-23T17:57:40.784419423Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":63,"issue_id":"bd-3mdhe","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Findings grouped by evidence pointer
- [ ] Duplicate findings detected (Jaccard similarity > 0.8)
- [ ] Contradictory positions identified
- [ ] MergeStats computed accurately

+{"id":"bd-3mdhe","title":"Task: Implement mechanical merging algorithms for findings/recommendations","description":"# Task: Mechanical Merging Algorithms

Algorithmic pre-processing before synthesis agent runs.

## Purpose
Because all mode outputs follow OutputSchema, we can do significant mechanical merging before the synthesis agent:
1. Reduce token cost for synthesis agent
2. Surface patterns algorithmically
3. Pre-group related items

## Implementation
```go
// internal/ensemble/merge.go
type MechanicalMerger struct {
    Outputs []ModeOutput
}

type MergeResult struct {
    GroupedFindings      []FindingGroup
    GroupedRisks         []RiskGroup
    GroupedRecommendations []RecommendationGroup
    IdentifiedConflicts  []PotentialConflict
    Statistics           MergeStats
}

type FindingGroup struct {
    EvidencePointer string
    Findings        []Finding
    Modes           []string
}

type PotentialConflict struct {
    Topic     string
    ModeA     string
    ModeB     string
    PositionA string
    PositionB string
}

type MergeStats struct {
    TotalFindings    int
    UniqueFindings   int
    DuplicateRate    float64
    ConflictCount    int
}

func NewMechanicalMerger(outputs []ModeOutput) *MechanicalMerger
func (m *MechanicalMerger) Merge() (*MergeResult, error)

// Grouping algorithms
func (m *MechanicalMerger) groupFindingsByEvidence() []FindingGroup
func (m *MechanicalMerger) groupRisksBySeverity() []RiskGroup
func (m *MechanicalMerger) groupRecommendationsByAction() []RecommendationGroup
func (m *MechanicalMerger) detectConflicts() []PotentialConflict

// Similarity functions
func jaccardSimilarity(a, b string) float64
func evidenceProximity(a, b string) float64  // Same file = high, nearby lines = medium
```

## Conflict Detection Heuristics
- Contradictory theses (opposite sentiment on same topic)
- Risk severity disagreement (same risk, different severity)
- Recommendation conflicts (do X vs don't do X)

 {"id":"bd-3mebp","title":"Task: Add concurrency safety to ntm assign watch and completion detection","description":"## Overview
Ensure thread-safety in watch mode and completion detection to prevent race conditions.

## Identified Race Conditions

### 1. Simultaneous Completion Events
Multiple agents complete at nearly the same time:
```
Time    Event
0ms     Agent 2 completes bd-abc
5ms     Agent 3 completes bd-def
10ms    Handler for bd-abc starts
15ms    Handler for bd-def starts  <-- Race!
```

**Solution:**
```go
type WatchLoop struct {
    completionCh   chan CompletionEvent
    assignmentLock sync.Mutex
}

func (w *WatchLoop) handleCompletions() {
    for event := range w.completionCh {
        w.assignmentLock.Lock()
        w.processCompletion(event)
        w.assignmentLock.Unlock()
    }
}
```

### 2. Assignment Store Updates During Query
While querying assignment store, another goroutine updates it:
```
Goroutine 1: Reading assignments to find idle agents
Goroutine 2: Updating assignment status to "completed"
```

**Solution:**
```go
// Already have mutex in AssignmentStore, ensure all methods use it:
func (s *AssignmentStore) GetIdleAgents() []Agent {
    s.mutex.RLock()
    defer s.mutex.RUnlock()
    // ... read logic
}

func (s *AssignmentStore) UpdateStatus(beadID string, status AssignmentStatus) {
    s.mutex.Lock()
    defer s.mutex.Unlock()
    // ... write logic
}
```

### 3. BV Cache Invalidation During Read
BV cache invalidated while strategy is reading recommendations:
```
Goroutine 1: Strategy reading from cache
Goroutine 2: Completion triggers cache invalidation
```

**Solution:**
```go
type BVClient struct {
    cache      *RecommendationCache
    cacheMutex sync.RWMutex
}

func (c *BVClient) GetRecommendations() []Recommendation {
    c.cacheMutex.RLock()
    cached := c.cache.Get()
    c.cacheMutex.RUnlock()
    
    if cached != nil {
        return cached
    }
    
    // Fetch fresh, then cache with write lock
    fresh := c.fetchFromBV()
    c.cacheMutex.Lock()
    c.cache.Set(fresh)
    c.cacheMutex.Unlock()
    return fresh
}
```

### 4. Completion Detection Race
Multiple detection methods find the same completion:
```
Method 1 (bead_poll): Detects completion
Method 2 (pattern_match): Also detects completion
Both try to emit CompletionEvent
```

**Solution:**
```go
type CompletionDetector struct {
    detected map[string]time.Time  // bead_id -> detected_at
    dedupMu  sync.Mutex
    dedupTTL time.Duration  // 5s default
}

func (d *CompletionDetector) emitIfNew(event CompletionEvent) bool {
    d.dedupMu.Lock()
    defer d.dedupMu.Unlock()
    
    if prev, ok := d.detected[event.BeadID]; ok {
        if time.Since(prev) < d.dedupTTL {
            return false  // Duplicate
        }
    }
    d.detected[event.BeadID] = time.Now()
    return true
}
```

### 5. File Reservation Transfer During Reassignment
Old reservation released, but new reservation fails:
```
1. Release old reservation  [SUCCESS]
2. Create new reservation   [FAIL - timeout]
3. Now no reservation exists
```

**Solution:**
```go
func transferReservation(from, to Agent, paths []string) error {
    // Create new reservation FIRST
    newRes, err := createReservation(to, paths)
    if err != nil {
        return err  // Old reservation still intact
    }
    
    // Then release old
    releaseReservation(from, paths)
    return nil
}
```

## Concurrency Testing
```go
func TestConcurrentCompletions(t *testing.T) {
    wg := sync.WaitGroup{}
    for i := 0; i < 10; i++ {
        wg.Add(1)
        go func(id int) {
            defer wg.Done()
            watchLoop.HandleCompletion(CompletionEvent{BeadID: fmt.Sprintf("bd-%d", id)})
        }(i)
    }
    wg.Wait()
    
    // Verify all completions processed without race
    // Run with: go test -race ./...
}
```

## Components to Update
- [ ] bd-92e1: Add explicit RWMutex usage documentation
- [ ] bd-25cq: Add dedup mechanism for detection methods
- [ ] bd-3b4yu: Add serialized completion handling
- [ ] bd-55pm: Add cache lock documentation
- [ ] bd-1ohy: Add reservation transfer order documentation

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All concurrent code paths identified
- [ ] Mutex/lock strategy documented for each component
- [ ] Race condition tests added
- [ ] `go test -race` passes
- [ ] Dedup mechanism prevents duplicate events
- [ ] Reservation transfer is atomic-safe

## Tests Required
- Unit test: Concurrent completion events (10+ goroutines)
- Unit test: Assignment store concurrent read/write
- Unit test: Cache invalidation during read
- Unit test: Dedup mechanism
- Integration test: Full watch loop with -race flag","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T22:07:30.196116659Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T04:00:07.204285755Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T04:00:07.204186157Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3mebp","depends_on_id":"bd-pjjk7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T22:07:30.225476461Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3mr38","title":"Task: Implement ntm --robot-giil wrapper","description":"## Overview
Implement `ntm --robot-giil-fetch=URL` command that wraps `giil` to download images from share URLs.

## Robot Mode API

```
ntm --robot-giil-fetch=URL
```

Example:
```bash
ntm --robot-giil-fetch="https://share.icloud.com/photos/abc123"
```

## Implementation Details

1. **Flag Registration** (root.go):
```go
rootCmd.Flags().StringVar(&robotGiilFetch, "robot-giil-fetch", "", 
    "Download image from share URL via giil. Required: URL")
```

2. **Handler Logic**:
   - Check giil availability via ToolRegistry
   - Execute: `giil <url>`
   - Parse output for downloaded file path
   - Stat the file for size
   - Return structured JSON

## JSON Output Schema
```json
{
  "success": true,
  "path": "/path/to/downloaded/image.jpg",
  "filename": "image.jpg", 
  "size_bytes": 1234567,
  "source_url": "https://share.icloud.com/..."
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Flag registered in root.go
- [ ] Handler in robot_giil.go
- [ ] Missing giil returns actionable error
- [ ] Works with iCloud share URLs
- [ ] JSON output includes all required fields","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

 {"id":"bd-y0uou","title":"E2E Tests: Robot Status Enrichments","description":"## What

End-to-end tests verifying status enrichments work with actual tmux sessions.

## Test Scenarios

### Scenario 1: Healthy Agent
1. Spawn session with single Claude agent
2. Wait for agent to initialize
3. Call --robot-status
4. Verify:
   - child_pid is valid PID
   - process_state is R or S
   - rate_limit_detected is false
   - memory_mb is reasonable (< 1000)
   - last_output_ts is recent
   - output_lines_since_last > 0

### Scenario 2: Rate Limited Agent
1. Spawn session with agent
2. Inject rate limit message into pane (tmux send-keys simulation)
3. Call --robot-status
4. Verify:
   - rate_limit_detected is true
   - rate_limit_match contains expected pattern

### Scenario 3: Stuck Agent
1. Spawn session with agent
2. Wait >5 minutes (or mock time)
3. Call --robot-status
4. Verify:
   - seconds_since_output > 300
   - last_output_ts is stale
   - output_lines_since_last == 0

### Scenario 4: Crashed Agent
1. Spawn session with agent
2. Kill agent process directly
3. Call --robot-status
4. Verify:
   - child_pid is null or process_state is Z
   - Appropriate status indicated

### Scenario 5: Large Swarm Performance
1. Spawn session with 16 agents
2. Call --robot-status
3. Verify:
   - All fields present for all panes
   - Response time < 2 seconds
   - No race conditions

### Scenario 6: Output Activity Tracking
1. Spawn session
2. Call --robot-status, record output_lines_since_last
3. Wait for agent to produce output
4. Call --robot-status again
5. Verify output_lines_since_last increased

## Test Infrastructure

```bash
# Setup
ntm spawn test-e2e --cc=3 --no-user

# Teardown
ntm kill test-e2e
```

## Logging Requirements

Each test must log:
- Test name and scenario at start
- All command outputs (JSON formatted)
- Timing information (start, end, duration)
- Pass/fail with detailed assertion info
- Full pane content on failure for debugging

Example log format:
```json
{"test": "healthy_agent", "step": 1, "action": "spawn", "result": "..."}
{"test": "healthy_agent", "step": 2, "action": "wait", "duration_ms": 5000}
{"test": "healthy_agent", "step": 3, "action": "status", "response": {...}}
{"test": "healthy_agent", "result": "PASS", "duration_ms": 7234}
```

## Dependencies

- Unit tests should pass first (bd-7cnnz)
- Requires actual tmux environment","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"Claude-4.5-Opus-mcp","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:20:08.801248285Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T03:05:56.637143324Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-y0uou","depends_on_id":"bd-1g9wv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:20:08.823912182Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-y0uou","depends_on_id":"bd-7cnnz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:20:42.674072331Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

-{"id":"bd-yn7qe","title":"Task: Refactor robot handlers into GetX/PrintX + service layer","description":"## Scope\nSplit internal/robot into data getters and printers, and extract reusable service layer for CLI/API.\n\n## Implementation\n- For each robot command: GetX() returns struct, PrintX() encodes JSON to stdout.\n- Services: SessionService, AgentService, OutputService, ContextService, ToolingService, ApprovalService, BeadsService, ScannerService (as applicable).\n- CLI handlers call services; REST handlers call the same services.\n\n## Logging\n- Structured logs for service entry/exit with session/agent IDs.\n\n## Tests\n- Unit: GetX() returns deterministic output with mock services.\n- Unit: Printers render correct JSON envelope.\n- Integration: CLI and REST share identical output structs.\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- No robot handler mixes data collection with JSON encoding.\n- Services are reusable across CLI/REST.\n- Tests validate envelope parity.\n","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T22:49:15.614832045Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-24T23:39:07.309878293Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":78,"issue_id":"bd-yn7qe","author":"opus-codex-1","text":"Progress update (opus-codex-1):

## Completed Refactoring
Refactored the following handlers into GetX/PrintX pattern:

**health.go:**
- GetHealth() + PrintHealth()
- GetSessionHealth() + PrintSessionHealth()

**session.go:**
- GetSave() + PrintSave()
- GetRestore() + PrintRestore()

**diagnose.go:**
- GetDiagnose() + PrintDiagnose()
- GetDiagnoseBrief() + PrintDiagnoseBrief()

**robot.go:**
- GetCASSStatus() + PrintCASSStatus()
- GetCASSSearch() + PrintCASSSearch() (with CASSSearchOptions struct)

## Pattern Established
- GetX() returns pointer to struct and nil error (errors encoded in RobotResponse)
- PrintX() calls GetX() then encodeJSON()
- Options structs used for complex parameter sets

## Tests Passing
All robot package tests pass (333s).

## Remaining Work
~80 more PrintX() handlers need refactoring. Suggest breaking into sub-tasks by file/category.

+{"id":"bd-yn7qe","title":"Task: Refactor robot handlers into GetX/PrintX + service layer","description":"## Scope\nSplit internal/robot into data getters and printers, and extract reusable service layer for CLI/API.\n\n## Implementation\n- For each robot command: GetX() returns struct, PrintX() encodes JSON to stdout.\n- Services: SessionService, AgentService, OutputService, ContextService, ToolingService, ApprovalService, BeadsService, ScannerService (as applicable).\n- CLI handlers call services; REST handlers call the same services.\n\n## Logging\n- Structured logs for service entry/exit with session/agent IDs.\n\n## Tests\n- Unit: GetX() returns deterministic output with mock services.\n- Unit: Printers render correct JSON envelope.\n- Integration: CLI and REST share identical output structs.\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- No robot handler mixes data collection with JSON encoding.\n- Services are reusable across CLI/REST.\n- Tests validate envelope parity.\n","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"WildIsland","created_at":"2026-01-23T22:49:15.614832045Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T16:53:49.603246373Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":78,"issue_id":"bd-yn7qe","author":"opus-codex-1","text":"Progress update (opus-codex-1):
